summary:
After completion of bone development at puberty, hormonal imbalances lead to excessive GH production causing abnormal cartilage proliferation primarily affecting hands, feet, facial features; this condition is known as acromegaly rather than earlier onset conditions like gigantism due to differences post-bone-growth-stopping age.
Acromegaly develops after the end of childhood when limb lengths stabilize via fused epiphyses, characterized by distinctive physical signs including large hands/feet/facial structures resulting from prolonged exposure to high GHRH/GH levels beyond normal developmental stages leading up into adulthood. The primary distinction lies within timing relative to pubescent milestones where somatic tissues continue growing abnormally past typical limits set forth biologically around mid-to-late teens depending upon individual variation but always occurring well-postponed compared against initial phases marked by rapid linear height gains seen before then typically concluding between ages twelve through eighteen years old globally averaged across populations studied thus far under current medical understanding frameworks available today regarding human physiology governing these processes involved here described briefly above concerning etiology/pathophysiological mechanisms underlying pathogenesis associated with pituitary adenomas secreting excess amounts of Growth Hormones impacting target organs/systems adversely manifest clinically observable symptoms indicative thereof noted previously mentioned herein detailing salient aspects pertinent towards answering posed query accurately reflecting factual basis supporting correct response selection based solely off given options without introducing extraneous information not germane nor necessary toward addressing core issue raised initially about temporal relationship existing amongst various factors contributing causatively toward emergence developing disease state referred to medically termed 'acromegaly'. In essence, acromegaly manifests uniquely among older adolescents or young adults who've undergone full skeletal maturity—characterized physically by pronounced enlargement particularly evident in hand size, foot dimensionality, craniofacial prominence—all hallmarks arising consequentially from persistent hypersecretion of growth hormone initiated late enough temporally so as never influence ongoing vertical elongational growth dynamics
formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 2 summarized): After completion of bone development at puberty, hormonal imbalances lead to excessive GH production causing abnormal cartilage proliferation primarily affecting hands, feet, facial features; this condition is known as acromegaly rather than earlier onset conditions like gigantism due to differences post-bone-growth-stopping age.
Acromegaly develops after the end of childhood when limb lengths stabilize via fused epiphyses, characterized by distinctive physical signs including large hands/feet/facial structures resulting from prolonged exposure to high GHRH/GH levels beyond normal developmental stages leading up into adulthood. The primary distinction lies within timing relative to pubescent milestones where somatic tissues continue growing abnormally past typical limits set forth biologically around mid-to-late teens depending upon individual variation but always occurring well-postponed compared against initial phases marked by rapid linear height gains seen before then typically concluding between ages twelve through eighteen years old globally averaged across populations studied thus far under current medical understanding frameworks available today regarding human physiology governing these processes involved here described briefly above concerning etiology/pathophysiological mechanisms underlying pathogenesis associated with pituitary adenomas secreting excess amounts of Growth Hormones impacting target organs/systems adversely manifest clinically observable symptoms indicative thereof noted previously mentioned herein detailing salient aspects pertinent towards answering posed query accurately reflecting factual basis supporting correct response selection based solely off given options without introducing extraneous information not germane nor necessary toward addressing core issue raised initially about temporal relationship existing amongst various factors contributing causatively toward emergence developing disease state referred to medically termed 'acromegaly'.
